Hiring teams

Video editor interview questions

Good editor interviews test process as much as taste: how someone handles footage, notes, deadlines, revisions, and unclear creative direction.

Ask about matched work

Start with examples similar to your content format. Ask what the editor owned, what they received, how much direction they had, and what they would change if they cut it again.

Test the workflow

Ask how they prefer to receive footage, organize assets, handle notes, export versions, and communicate risk. A beautiful reel can still fail if the workflow is chaotic.

Clarify turnaround and revisions

Ask what timeline is realistic for your source footage volume, how many revision rounds are included, what slows them down, and what they need from you before the first cut.

Need an editor?

Send a hiring brief and start the early matching queue.
